# TheMapScraper — Complete Reference

> Google Maps Scraper — Extract business leads from Google Maps instantly. No extensions, no API keys, no code.

## Product overview

TheMapScraper (also known as a map scraper) is a web-based tool that extracts business data from Google Maps search results. Users type a search query (like "restaurants in Miami" or "dentists in London"), and the tool scrapes all matching business listings into a structured dataset. The output is a clean CSV file ready for CRM import, cold outreach, or market analysis.

The key differentiator is simplicity: no browser extension to install, no API keys to configure, no coding required. It works directly in the browser — type a search, click extract, download CSV.

## Target users

- Sales teams and SDRs building B2B prospect lists
- Digital marketing agencies prospecting local business clients
- Real estate agents sourcing property leads
- Recruiters finding companies to pitch
- Market researchers analyzing local business landscapes
- SEO agencies auditing local competitors

## Data fields extracted

| Field | Description | Example |
|---|---|---|
| Business name | Official listing name | "Joe's Pizza" |
| Phone number | Primary contact phone | "+1 212-555-0123" |
| Email address | Contact email when available | "info@joespizza.com" |
| Address | Full street address | "123 Main St, New York, NY 10001" |
| Website | Business website URL | "https://joespizza.com" |
| Google rating | Star rating (1-5) | "4.5" |
| Review count | Total Google reviews | "234" |
| Category | Google Maps category | "Pizza Restaurant" |
| Opening hours | Weekly schedule | "Mon-Fri 11am-10pm" |
| Google Maps URL | Direct link to listing | "https://maps.google.com/..." |
| Coordinates | Latitude and longitude | "40.7128, -74.0060" |

## Comparison with alternatives

### TheMapScraper vs Apify
- Apify requires creating an "actor", configuring JSON input parameters, and understanding their platform. It's built for developers.
- TheMapScraper: type a query, click extract, download CSV. Built for non-technical users.
- Apify pricing is usage-based and can be unpredictable. TheMapScraper has simple subscription tiers.

### TheMapScraper vs Outscraper
- Outscraper charges per record with complex volume-based pricing that's hard to predict upfront.
- TheMapScraper offers flat subscription pricing — you know exactly what you'll pay.
- Both extract similar data fields from Google Maps.

### TheMapScraper vs Chrome extensions (Instant Data Scraper, etc.)
- Chrome extensions break frequently when Google Maps updates its DOM structure.
- Extensions require installation, browser permissions, and keeping the tab open during scraping.
- TheMapScraper runs server-side — more reliable, doesn't tie up your browser, works even when Google Maps changes its frontend.

### TheMapScraper vs PhantomBuster
- PhantomBuster is a general-purpose automation platform — Google Maps is just one of many "phantoms."
- TheMapScraper is purpose-built for Google Maps scraping, so the UX is simpler and more focused.
- PhantomBuster has a steep learning curve with workflows, chains, and scheduling concepts.

### TheMapScraper vs building your own scraper (Python/Selenium)
- Building a custom scraper requires Python knowledge, handling anti-bot measures, proxy rotation, and ongoing maintenance.
- TheMapScraper handles all of that — users just type a query and get results.
- Custom scrapers break when Google changes their page structure. TheMapScraper maintains the scraping infrastructure.

## How it works

1. Go to themapscraper.com
2. Enter any Google Maps search query (e.g. "plumbers in Chicago", "gyms near downtown LA")
3. The tool searches Google Maps and extracts all business listings matching your query
4. Review the extracted data in the browser
5. Download a clean CSV file with all fields
6. Import into your CRM, spreadsheet, or outreach tool

## Common use cases with examples

**Lead generation for sales teams**: An SDR selling POS systems to restaurants scrapes "restaurants in Austin TX" to build a prospect list of 500+ restaurants with phone numbers and websites for cold outreach.

**Agency client prospecting**: A digital marketing agency scrapes "businesses without website in [city]" to find potential clients who need web design services.

**Real estate lead sourcing**: A real estate agent scrapes "property management companies in Miami" to find potential partners and referral sources.

**Competitor analysis**: A local business scrapes their category in their city to see all competitors, their ratings, and review counts.

**Market research**: A franchise evaluating expansion scrapes multiple cities to compare business density and competition levels.

## Pricing

Free tier: 50 leads per month, no credit card required. Paid plans for higher volumes. Visit https://www.themapscraper.com/pricing/ for current pricing.
